old_claim.note: Naval Logbooks provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The Board tested longitude methods on voyages and preserved logs and voyage records as evaluation evidence.

source_shape.source_locator: Collection introduction: to test longitude methods and make measurements, the Board hired astronomers for Pacific voyages including Cook, Vancouver, and Flinders; the archive includes logbooks from Cook's voyages and logs kept by William Gooch.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source documents Board testing voyages and preserved voyage logs as part of longitude-method evaluation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports an enabling edge because voyage logs were evaluation records for trials and measurements, not just generic naval paperwork.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: longitude_prize_systems remains enabled by naval_logbooks, now with source-backed evidence.
would_reject_if: The node is later narrowed to statute drafting without voyage trials.
A stronger source shows Board testing did not rely on voyage logs or expedition records.
The naval_logbooks node is narrowed away from recorded observations during long voyages.
short_reason: Board testing voyages and logs directly support evaluation of longitude methods.
